labs.initialMonitoring: daily in patients with advanced CKD
labs.maintenanceFrequency: daily glycemic monitoring for hypoglycemia prevention
labs.actionRequired: dose reduction when used with SGLT2i or GLP-1 RA
labs.clinicalContext: Risk of hypoglycemia is high in patients with advanced CKD who are treated by glucose-lowering agents that raise blood insulin levels. Without daily glycemic monitoring, it is often difficult to avoid hypoglycemic episodes

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: Higher hypoglycemia risk in CKD due to reduced renal clearance of insulin. Consider higher HbA1c target (e.g., <7.5% or <8%) for patients at higher risk for hypoglycemia with low GFR. Suitable for patients with eGFR <15 ml/min per 1.73 m2 or on dialysis.
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: Risk of hypoglycemia is high in patients with advanced CKD who are treated by glucose-lowering agents that raise blood insulin levels (exogenous insulin, sulfonylureas, meglitinides). Therefore, without daily glycemic monitoring, it is often difficult to avoid hypoglycemic episodes

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease
